title: Chocolate on Polyester
stainChemistry: Chocolate is four-part: cocoa butter (fat) + milk protein (protein) + cocoa tannin (pigment) + sugar. On polyester, the OLEOPHILIC fiber holds onto cocoa butter tenaciously — Dawn / NSD step is mandatory before peroxide can reach the pigment. Standard protocol: scrape solid bulk → cold water sugar flush → Dawn (oil-strip) → enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot) for milk protein → 3% peroxide for tannin → vinegar rinse → cool wash. Tannin rules apply (no alkaline alone — locks tannin). Polyester rules apply (no hot water / hot dryer until clear — yellows fiber over time AND sets residual chocolate).
whyThisWorks: Polyester tolerates the standard chocolate combo protocol. Order matters: oil-strip BEFORE oxidizer (oil blocks peroxide on oleophilic polyester especially). Warm enzyme handles milk protein without setting it. Vinegar keeps residual tannin light. Cool wash + air-dry preserves polyester long-term.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / kid clothes / workout wear. Fresh chocolate = quick win. Hot-dried chocolate = permanent. Dark chocolate harder than milk; white chocolate is mostly fat (no tannin) — slightly easier.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Ice bag on chocolate 5 min. Lift solid pieces with flat edge of plastic card.
Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK 1–2 min. Sugar flushes out fast; cocoa butter stays.
Dawn into the spot with soft toothbrush 5–10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn step is mandatory or the cocoa butter blocks peroxide from reaching tannin.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz in WARM (<100°F) water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme in WARM water — bath temp, not hot. Damp cloth on spot 15 min. Digests milk protein.
Hidden-area test on dyed polyester. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.
Vinegar rinse 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps tannin light.
Cool wash per care label. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer — heat permanently sets residual chocolate on polyester.

steps: 3. Dawn + soft toothbrush — oil-strip BEFORE peroxide.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold for tannin / sugar flush; warm (<100°F) for enzyme; never hot wash
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets tannin + milk protein + yellows polyester
NEVER alkaline cleaners alone on tannin — darkens permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear — heat permanent
Oil-strip BEFORE peroxide — polyester is oleophilic

homeSolutions.instruction: Chocolate is four-part. On polyester, two rules: (1) ORDER matters — Dawn (oil-strip) BEFORE peroxide, because polyester loves oil and oil blocks peroxide from reaching tannin, and (2) NO hot water / hot dryer until fully clear — heat sets residual chocolate AND yellows polyester.
Ice bag on chocolate 5 minutes to firm cocoa butter. Lift solid pieces with the flat edge of a plastic card.
Flip the garment inside out. Run cold tap water through the BACK of the stain for 1–2 minutes. Sugar flushes out fast; cocoa butter stays for the next step.
Mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cool water. Work into the spot with a soft toothbrush 5–10 minutes. Polyester GRABS oil — without this step, peroxide can't reach the tannin underneath. Cool rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Cream / Milk Chocolate? Warm (Not Hot) Enzyme
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve OxiClean Versatile or Biz in WARM water — bath temperature, under 100°F. NOT hot. Damp cloth 15 min. Digests milk protein. Skip for dark chocolate without milk.
3% drugstore peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Hidden-area test on dyed polyester first. Skip if test fails.
1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water, 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps any residual tannin light.
Cool wash per care label. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer. Residual chocolate + dryer heat = permanent on polyester.

steps: 4. Dawn + soft toothbrush — MANDATORY oil-strip — 5–10 min.
6. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, hidden test on dyed.
8. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect before dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual chocolate, if alkaline was applied (tannin darkened), or if oil-strip step was skipped (polyester locks oil).
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets all three components
NEVER alkaline cleaners on tannin — darkens
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
ORDER matters — oil-strip BEFORE peroxide on polyester
NEVER mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia

neverDo: Never hot dryer on residual chocolate
Never skip oil-strip step on polyester
Never mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn step mandatory
Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester tolerates 3% peroxide on dyed (with hidden test)

materialWarnings: No hot water / hot dryer — sets chocolate + yellows polyester
Polyester loves oil — Dawn is mandatory

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual chocolate, alkaline applied, oil-strip skipped, set-in stain weeks old.
escalation.whatToTell: "Chocolate on polyester, [milk / dark / white], age [hours / days], home-treated in order: ice-lift + cold back-flush + Dawn oil-strip + enzyme + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline. [Residual / hot-dried]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / overnight / older?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Dark chocolate has more tannin — harder. White chocolate mostly cocoa butter — easier. Pre-applied OxiClean = tannin darkened permanently. Pre-applied hot dryer = permanent.
